key_points: The story 'The Beggar' is written by Anton Chekhov
Lushkoff is a beggar who lies to gain sympathy
Sergei is a lawyer who advises Lushkoff to work honestly
Olga is Sergei’s cook who secretly helps Lushkoff
Lushkoff reforms his life and becomes a notary
characters: Lushkoff – A dishonest beggar who later reforms
Sergei – A lawyer who tries to guide Lushkoff
themes: Compassion can change a person’s life
literary_devices: Character development – Lushkoff changes from a beggar to a respectable man
Realism – The story reflects real-life social problems like poverty
exam_tips: Remember Olga’s role in Lushkoff’s reform
Focus on the dignity of labour theme
Use examples of compassion from the story
